result.stdout:
Description:
  The Coyote systematic testing tool.
  
  Learn how to use Coyote at https://aka.ms/learn-coyote.
  Learn what is new at https://aka.ms/coyote-what-is-new.

Usage:
  coyote [command] [options]

Options:
  -v, --verbosity <LEVEL>  Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]
  --version                Show version information
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

Commands:
  test <PATH>                 Run tests using the Coyote systematic testing engine.
                              Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-test.
  replay <PATH> <TRACE_FILE>  Replay bugs that Coyote discovered during systematic testing.
                              Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-replay.
  rewrite <PATH>              Rewrite your assemblies to inject logic that allows Coyote to take control of the execution during systematic testing.
                              Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-rewrite.

commands.payload:
Description:
  The Coyote systematic testing tool.
  
  Learn how to use Coyote at https://aka.ms/learn-coyote.
  Learn what is new at https://aka.ms/coyote-what-is-new.

Usage:

Options:
  -v, --verbosity <LEVEL>  Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]
  --version                Show version information
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

Commands:
  test <PATH>                 Run tests using the Coyote systematic testing engine.
                              Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-test.
  replay <PATH> <TRACE_FILE>  Replay bugs that Coyote discovered during systematic testing.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Replay bugs that Coyote discovered during systematic testing.
  Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-replay.

Usage:
  coyote replay <path> <trace> [options]

Arguments:
  <PATH>        Path to the assembly (*.dll, *.exe) to replay.
  <TRACE_FILE>  *.trace file containing the execution path to replay.

Options:
  -m, --method <METHOD>    Suffix of the test method to execute.
  -b, --break              Attaches the debugger and adds a breakpoint when an assertion fails.
  -o, --outdir <PATH>      Output directory for emitting reports. This can be an absolute path or relative to current directory.
  --console                Log all runtime messages to the console unless overridden by a custom ILogger.
  -v, --verbosity <LEVEL>  Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Replay bugs that Coyote discovered during systematic testing.
  Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-replay.

Usage:

Arguments:
  <PATH>        Path to the assembly (*.dll, *.exe) to replay.
  <TRACE_FILE>  *.trace file containing the execution path to replay.

Options:
  -m, --method <METHOD>    Suffix of the test method to execute.
  -b, --break              Attaches the debugger and adds a breakpoint when an assertion fails.
  -o, --outdir <PATH>      Output directory for emitting reports. This can be an absolute path or relative to current directory.
  --console                Log all runtime messages to the console unless overridden by a custom ILogger.
  -v, --verbosity <LEVEL>  Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Rewrite your assemblies to inject logic that allows Coyote to take control of the execution during systematic testing.
  Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-rewrite.

Usage:
  coyote rewrite <path> [options]

Arguments:
  <PATH>  Path to the assembly (*.dll, *.exe) to rewrite or to a JSON rewriting configuration file.

Options:
  --rewrite-memory-locations        Rewrite memory locations (such as field loads and stores) for race checking. [default: True]
  --rewrite-concurrent-collections  Rewrite concurrent collections for race checking. [default: True]
  --dump-il                         Dumps the original and rewritten IL in JSON for debugging purposes. [default: False]
  --dump-il-diff                    Dumps the IL diff in JSON for debugging purposes. [default: False]
  -v, --verbosity <LEVEL>           Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Rewrite your assemblies to inject logic that allows Coyote to take control of the execution during systematic testing.
  Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-rewrite.

Usage:
  coyote rewrite <path> [options]

Arguments:
  <PATH>  Path to the assembly (*.dll, *.exe) to rewrite or to a JSON rewriting configuration file.

Options:
  --rewrite-memory-locations        Rewrite memory locations (such as field loads and stores) for race checking. [default: True]
  --rewrite-concurrent-collections  Rewrite concurrent collections for race checking. [default: True]
  --dump-il                         Dumps the original and rewritten IL in JSON for debugging purposes. [default: False]
  --dump-il-diff                    Dumps the IL diff in JSON for debugging purposes. [default: False]
  -v, --verbosity <LEVEL>           Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Run tests using the Coyote systematic testing engine.
  Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-test.

Usage:
  coyote test <path> [options]

Arguments:
  <PATH>  Path to the assembly (*.dll, *.exe) to test.

Options:
  -m, --method <METHOD>                                   Suffix of the test method to execute.
  -i, --iterations <ITERATIONS>                           Number of testing iterations to run. [default: 1]
  -t, --timeout <TIMEOUT>                                 Timeout in seconds after which no more testing iterations will run (disabled by default). [default: 0]
  -s, --strategy <STRATEGY>                               Set exploration strategy to use during testing. The exploration strategy controls all scheduling decisions and nondeterministic choices. Note that explicitly setting this value disables the default exploration mode that uses a tuned portfolio of strategies. Allowed values are random, probabilistic, prioritization, fair-prioritization, delay-bounding, fair-delay-bounding, q-learning. [default: random]
  -sv, --strategy-value <VALUE>                           Set exploration strategy specific value. Supported strategies (and values): probabilistic (probability of deviating from a scheduled operation), (fair-)prioritization (maximum number of priority changes per iteration), (fair-)delay-bounding (maximum number of delays per iteration).
  --portfolio-mode <MODE>                                 Set the portfolio mode to use during testing. Portfolio mode uses a tuned portfolio of strategies, instead of the default or user-specified strategy. If fair mode is enabled, then the portfolio will upgrade any unfair strategies to fair, by adding a fair execution suffix after the the max fair scheduling steps bound has been reached. Allowed values are fair, unfair. [default: fair]
  -ms, --max-steps <STEPS>                                Max scheduling steps (i.e. decisions) to be explored during testing. Choosing value 'STEPS' sets 'STEPS' unfair max-steps and 'STEPS*10' fair steps.
  --max-fair-steps <STEPS>                                Max fair scheduling steps (i.e. decisions) to be explored during testing. Used by exploration strategies that perform fair scheduling. [default: 100000]
  --max-unfair-steps <STEPS>                              Max unfair scheduling steps (i.e. decisions) to be explored during testing. Used by exploration strategies that perform unfair scheduling. [default: 10000]
  --fuzz                                                  Use systematic fuzzing instead of controlled testing.
  -c, --coverage                                          Generate coverage reports if supported for the programming model used by the test.
  --schedule-coverage                                     Output a '.coverage.schedule.txt' file containing scheduling coverage information during testing.
  --serialize-coverage                                    Output a '.coverage.ser' file that contains the serialized coverage information.
  --actor-graph                                           Output a DGML graph that visualizes the failing actor execution path if a bug is found.
  --xml-trace                                             Output an XML formatted runtime log file.
  --reduce-execution-trace-cycles                         Enable execution trace cycle detection and reduction heuristics.
  --partial-order-sampling                                Enable partial-order sampling based on 'READ' and 'WRITE' scheduling points.
  --seed <VALUE>                                          Specify the random value generator seed.
  --liveness-temperature-threshold <THRESHOLD>            Specify the threshold (in number of steps) that triggers a liveness bug. [default: 50000]
  --timeout-delay <DELAY>                                 Specify the frequency of timeouts (not a unit of time). [default: 10]
  --deadlock-timeout <TIMEOUT>                            Specify how much time (in ms) to wait before reporting a potential deadlock. [default: 1000]
  --max-fuzz-delay <DELAY>                                Specify the maximum time (in number of busy loops) an operation might get delayed during systematic fuzzing. [default: 1000]
  --resolve-uncontrolled-concurrency-attempts <ATTEMPTS>  Specify how many times to try resolve each instance of uncontrolled concurrency. [default: 10]
  --resolve-uncontrolled-concurrency-delay <DELAY>        Specify how much time (in number of busy loops) to wait between each attempt to resolve each instance of uncontrolled concurrency. [default: 1000]
  --skip-trace-analysis                                   Disable execution graph analysis during testing.
  --skip-potential-deadlocks                              Only report a deadlock when the runtime can fully determine that it is genuine and not due to partially-controlled concurrency.
  --skip-collection-races                                 Disable exploration of race conditions when accessing collections.
  --skip-lock-races                                       Disable exploration of race conditions when accessing lock-based synchronization primitives.
  --skip-atomic-races                                     Disable exploration of race conditions when performing atomic operations.
  --skip-volatile-races                                   Disable exploration of race conditions when performing volatile operations.
  --enable-memory-access-races                            Enable exploration of race conditions at memory-access locations.
  --enable-control-flow-races                             Enable exploration of race conditions at control-flow branching locations.
  --fuzzing-fallback                                      Enable automatic fallback to systematic fuzzing upon detecting uncontrolled concurrency.
  --partial-control <MODE>                                Set the partial controlled mode to use during testing. If set to 'concurrency' then only concurrency can be partially controlled. If set to 'data' then only data non-determinism can be partially controlled. If set to 'none' then partially controlled testing is disabled. By default, both concurrency and data non-determinism can be partially controlled. Allowed values are none, concurrency, data.
  --no-repro                                              Disable bug trace repro to ignore uncontrolled concurrency errors.
  --log-uncontrolled-invocation-stack-traces              Enable logging the stack traces of uncontrolled invocations detected during testing.
  --fail-on-max-steps                                     Reaching the specified max-steps is treated as a bug.
  -b, --break                                             Attach the debugger and add a breakpoint when an assertion fails.
  -o, --outdir <PATH>                                     Output directory for emitting reports. This can be an absolute path or relative to current directory.
  --console                                               Log all runtime messages to the console unless overridden by a custom ILogger.
  -v, --verbosity <LEVEL>                                 Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]
  -?, -h, --help                                          Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Run tests using the Coyote systematic testing engine.
  Learn more at https://aka.ms/coyote-test.

Usage:
  coyote test <path> [options]

Arguments:
  <PATH>  Path to the assembly (*.dll, *.exe) to test.

Options:
  -m, --method <METHOD>                                   Suffix of the test method to execute.
  -i, --iterations <ITERATIONS>                           Number of testing iterations to run. [default: 1]
  -t, --timeout <TIMEOUT>                                 Timeout in seconds after which no more testing iterations will run (disabled by default). [default: 0]
  -s, --strategy <STRATEGY>                               Set exploration strategy to use during testing. The exploration strategy controls all scheduling decisions and nondeterministic choices. Note that explicitly setting this value disables the default exploration mode that uses a tuned portfolio of strategies. Allowed values are random, probabilistic, prioritization, fair-prioritization, delay-bounding, fair-delay-bounding, q-learning. [default: random]
  -sv, --strategy-value <VALUE>                           Set exploration strategy specific value. Supported strategies (and values): probabilistic (probability of deviating from a scheduled operation), (fair-)prioritization (maximum number of priority changes per iteration), (fair-)delay-bounding (maximum number of delays per iteration).
  --portfolio-mode <MODE>                                 Set the portfolio mode to use during testing. Portfolio mode uses a tuned portfolio of strategies, instead of the default or user-specified strategy. If fair mode is enabled, then the portfolio will upgrade any unfair strategies to fair, by adding a fair execution suffix after the the max fair scheduling steps bound has been reached. Allowed values are fair, unfair. [default: fair]
  -ms, --max-steps <STEPS>                                Max scheduling steps (i.e. decisions) to be explored during testing. Choosing value 'STEPS' sets 'STEPS' unfair max-steps and 'STEPS*10' fair steps.
  --max-fair-steps <STEPS>                                Max fair scheduling steps (i.e. decisions) to be explored during testing. Used by exploration strategies that perform fair scheduling. [default: 100000]
  --max-unfair-steps <STEPS>                              Max unfair scheduling steps (i.e. decisions) to be explored during testing. Used by exploration strategies that perform unfair scheduling. [default: 10000]
  --fuzz                                                  Use systematic fuzzing instead of controlled testing.
  -c, --coverage                                          Generate coverage reports if supported for the programming model used by the test.
  --schedule-coverage                                     Output a '.coverage.schedule.txt' file containing scheduling coverage information during testing.
  --serialize-coverage                                    Output a '.coverage.ser' file that contains the serialized coverage information.
  --actor-graph                                           Output a DGML graph that visualizes the failing actor execution path if a bug is found.
  --xml-trace                                             Output an XML formatted runtime log file.
  --reduce-execution-trace-cycles                         Enable execution trace cycle detection and reduction heuristics.
  --partial-order-sampling                                Enable partial-order sampling based on 'READ' and 'WRITE' scheduling points.
  --seed <VALUE>                                          Specify the random value generator seed.
  --liveness-temperature-threshold <THRESHOLD>            Specify the threshold (in number of steps) that triggers a liveness bug. [default: 50000]
  --timeout-delay <DELAY>                                 Specify the frequency of timeouts (not a unit of time). [default: 10]
  --deadlock-timeout <TIMEOUT>                            Specify how much time (in ms) to wait before reporting a potential deadlock. [default: 1000]
  --max-fuzz-delay <DELAY>                                Specify the maximum time (in number of busy loops) an operation might get delayed during systematic fuzzing. [default: 1000]
  --resolve-uncontrolled-concurrency-attempts <ATTEMPTS>  Specify how many times to try resolve each instance of uncontrolled concurrency. [default: 10]
  --resolve-uncontrolled-concurrency-delay <DELAY>        Specify how much time (in number of busy loops) to wait between each attempt to resolve each instance of uncontrolled concurrency. [default: 1000]
  --skip-trace-analysis                                   Disable execution graph analysis during testing.
  --skip-potential-deadlocks                              Only report a deadlock when the runtime can fully determine that it is genuine and not due to partially-controlled concurrency.
  --skip-collection-races                                 Disable exploration of race conditions when accessing collections.
  --skip-lock-races                                       Disable exploration of race conditions when accessing lock-based synchronization primitives.
  --skip-atomic-races                                     Disable exploration of race conditions when performing atomic operations.
  --skip-volatile-races                                   Disable exploration of race conditions when performing volatile operations.
  --enable-memory-access-races                            Enable exploration of race conditions at memory-access locations.
  --enable-control-flow-races                             Enable exploration of race conditions at control-flow branching locations.
  --fuzzing-fallback                                      Enable automatic fallback to systematic fuzzing upon detecting uncontrolled concurrency.
  --partial-control <MODE>                                Set the partial controlled mode to use during testing. If set to 'concurrency' then only concurrency can be partially controlled. If set to 'data' then only data non-determinism can be partially controlled. If set to 'none' then partially controlled testing is disabled. By default, both concurrency and data non-determinism can be partially controlled. Allowed values are none, concurrency, data.
  --no-repro                                              Disable bug trace repro to ignore uncontrolled concurrency errors.
  --log-uncontrolled-invocation-stack-traces              Enable logging the stack traces of uncontrolled invocations detected during testing.
  --fail-on-max-steps                                     Reaching the specified max-steps is treated as a bug.
  -b, --break                                             Attach the debugger and add a breakpoint when an assertion fails.
  -o, --outdir <PATH>                                     Output directory for emitting reports. This can be an absolute path or relative to current directory.
  --console                                               Log all runtime messages to the console unless overridden by a custom ILogger.
  -v, --verbosity <LEVEL>                                 Enable verbosity with an optional verbosity level. Allowed values are none, error, warning, info, debug, exhaustive. Skipping the argument sets the verbosity level to 'info'. [default: error]
  -?, -h, --help                                          Show help and usage information
